commit.message:
Simplify database: drop songs table, serve from constants by ESC year, store country codes in votes (#25)

Songs are static per ESC year and don't belong in the database. Votes
stored English country names, making them fragile to typos. All old data
is eradicated (not yet in production).

### Schema changes
- Migration 006: deletes all existing data, drops `songs` table
- Remaining tables: `games` (with `escYear`), `voters`, `votes`
- `votes.country` now stores ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 codes (e.g. `"MDA"`)
instead of `"Moldova"`

### Constants restructure
- `ESC_SONGS` → `ESC_SONGS_BY_YEAR` with `as const satisfies
Record<number, readonly UnsafeSong[]>` to preserve literal types, plus
`code` field on each entry
- `CountryCode` union type derived from the data: `(typeof
ESC_SONGS_BY_YEAR)[keyof typeof ESC_SONGS_BY_YEAR][number]["code"]`
- `Song` interface has `code: CountryCode` — the strongly typed default
used by all consumers
- `UnsafeSong` is a private interface with `code: string`, used only for
the `satisfies` constraint (avoids circular dependency with
`CountryCode`)
- `getSongsForYear(year)` helper replaces DB queries, returns `readonly
Song[]` so `CountryCode` flows naturally to all consumers without type
casts
- `Song` interface: removed `id` and `runningOrder` (no longer DB rows)

### Data layer
- `createGame`: no longer inserts 35 song rows in batches
- `deleteGame`: no longer deletes from `songs`
- `getAssumedVotesForVoter` / `getResultsByScore`: take `escYear` param,
match votes by `song.code`

### UI components
- Pages fetch songs via `getSongsForYear(game.escYear)` instead of
`getSongs(gameId)`
- `RatingButtons` accepts `countryCode` prop typed as `CountryCode` for
vote persistence, `country` for display
- `SongInfo.code` in `DashboardControls` is strictly typed as
`CountryCode` union literal
- `VoteSongList.songs` uses `Song` so `CountryCode` propagates without
casts
- Vote lookups key on `song.code` instead of `song.country`

### Testing
- Added unit tests for `getSongsForYear`, song data integrity (unique
codes/names, required fields, 3-letter ISO format), and `CountryCode`
type safety
- Added `unit` job to CI workflow to run `pnpm run test:unit` on every
push/PR

```ts
// Before: songs duplicated into DB per game
const songs = await getSongs(game.id);
voteRecord[v.country]   // "Moldova"

// After: songs from constants, votes keyed by code
const songs = getSongsForYear(game.escYear);
voteRecord[v.country]   // "MDA"
```

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: valscion <482561+valscion@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
security: fix all open Dependabot + CodeQL alerts

Dependabot fixes:
- waitress: 2.1.2 → 3.0.0 (Docker/3.8), 3.0.2 (host/3.10)
  Fixes CRITICAL CVE (race condition) and HIGH CVE (DoS)
  Note: Docker 3.0.0 is max for Python 3.8, health server is
  internal-only so residual risk is minimal
- requests: 2.31.0 → 2.32.4 (Docker/3.8), 2.33.0 (host/3.10)
  Fixes .netrc leak, verify=False, temp file reuse CVEs
- flask: 3.0.3 (Docker/3.8), 3.1.3 (host/3.10)
  Fixes Vary: Cookie header CVE

CodeQL fixes:
- provision.py: Mask hostname and device_id in log output
  to avoid clear-text logging of sensitive data

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Add initial implementation of Bot Framework library

- Created pyproject.toml for project configuration and dependencies.
- Implemented core components including BotApplication, BotAuth, and TokenManager.
- Added HTTP client for handling requests to Bot Framework services.
- Developed conversation management features in ConversationClient.
- Introduced core activity models for handling messages and activities.
- Implemented middleware support for processing activities.
- Added user token management functionality with UserTokenClient.
- Created unit tests for BotApplication and CoreActivity functionalities.

* refactor: update Python directory structure to use 'python/packages/botas'

* feat: Implement Bot Framework middleware and client libraries

- Add bot-auth-middleware for validating Bot Framework Bearer tokens.
- Introduce bot-http-client for making authenticated HTTP requests to the Bot Framework REST API.
- Create conversation-client for managing conversations, sending activities, and retrieving members.
- Define core-activity types and utility functions for activity handling.
- Implement i-turn-middleware interface for cross-cutting concerns in activity processing.
- Develop user-token-client for managing user tokens and OAuth sign-in flows.
- Add logger utility for consistent logging across the library.
- Implement token-manager for acquiring Bot Framework access tokens using MSAL.
- Create tests for core-activity functionality.
- Add TypeScript configuration for the project.

* refactor: update folder structure and paths across documentation and project files

* refactor: reorder imports in bot_application.py and __init__.py for consistency

* refactor: reorganize dotnet project structure and migrate ChannelAccount and Conversation classes to CoreActivity namespace

* feat: add SVG logo for botas SDK

feat: storage policy for Jetson .engine upload via anon key

Migration 019 adds 3 policies on storage.objects for the 'models' bucket:
- INSERT: anon can upload to engines/* (new engine after conversion)
- UPDATE: anon can upsert engines/* (re-conversion overwrites)
- SELECT: anon can read engines/* (download on fresh setup)

All scoped to 'engines/' prefix only — does not affect model files
managed by admin dashboard.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: upload .engine files to Supabase Storage after TensorRT conversion

After converting ONNX → TensorRT, the .engine file is uploaded to
Supabase Storage as a device-specific backup. Storage path includes
hardware model and TensorRT version for clear identification:

  models/engines/{jetson-orin-nano}_trt{10.3}/{leaf_type}_student.engine

Device info auto-detected from /proc/device-tree/model and dpkg.
Upload is best-effort — failure doesn't block setup.

NOTE: .engine files are NOT portable across GPU architectures.
Each device must either convert ONNX locally or download an engine
built on identical hardware + TensorRT version.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(lint): cover locate_json_key None path and check_file malformed JSON

Add tests for two previously uncovered branches in the hook lint rules:

1. hook_legacy_event.rs line 101: the Err(_) arm in check_file() that
   silently skips malformed JSON was never exercised.

2. hook_legacy_event.rs / hook_unknown_event.rs: locate_json_key()
   returns None when the JSON key in the raw file is written using
   a Unicode escape (e.g. "St\u006fp" → "Stop"). serde_json resolves
   the escape when parsing, but locate_json_key searches the raw bytes
   for the literal key string and cannot find it — resulting in a
   diagnostic with line=None, col=None, end_col=None.

Both hook_legacy_event.rs and hook_unknown_event.rs now report 100%
branch coverage.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: GUI service X11 auth — add XAUTHORITY + xhost for display access

systemctl start agrikd-gui and desktop shortcut failed silently because:
1. XAUTHORITY env var was missing — X11 server rejected connections
2. xhost didn't grant access to the agrikd service user

Now setup_jetson.sh:
- Auto-detects XAUTHORITY path (gdm, home dir variants)
- Runs xhost +SI:localuser:agrikd for X11 access
- Sets QT_QPA_PLATFORM=xcb for PyQt5
- Adds /run/user to ReadWritePaths for X11 socket access

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: auto-resolve CWD to install root for engine path resolution

Both gui_app.py and main.py now auto-detect the install root on startup:
- Host layout: /opt/agrikd/app/main.py → CWD = /opt/agrikd/
- Docker layout: /app/main.py → CWD = /app/

This ensures relative paths in config.json (models/*, config/*, data/*)
resolve correctly regardless of where the script is launched from.

Previously, running 'python3 /opt/agrikd/app/gui_app.py' from any
directory other than /opt/agrikd/ would fail to find engine files.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Reorganize project structure and fix image references

Phase 1-8 Complete:
- Create new directory structure (pages, _drafts, content/posts, content/images)
- Move pages: videos.markdown -> pages/videos.md, about.markdown -> pages/about.md
- Reorganize images from flat /images/ to /content/images/shared/
- Create new layouts: _layouts/post.html, _layouts/page.html
- Update _config.yml with defaults for post and page layouts
- Fix all image references in posts (/images/ -> /content/images/shared/)
- Update navigation links to new pages/ folder
- Build verified: all posts and pages render correctly

New Structure:
- _posts/          - Blog post markdown (clean, layout-only)
- pages/           - Static pages (About, Videos)
- _drafts/         - Work-in-progress posts
- content/posts/   - Future: post-specific assets
- content/images/  - Centralized image storage
- _layouts/        - post.html, page.html for consistent styling

Benefits:
✓ Scalable: Easy to add 50+ more posts
✓ Organized: Images with content in centralized location
✓ Maintainable: Clear folder hierarchy
✓ Future-proof: Ready for galleries, attachments, etc.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(#195): switch to PendingIntent OAuth delivery for Samsung compatibility (#198)

* fix(#195): switch to PendingIntent OAuth delivery for Samsung compatibility

Replace ActivityResultLauncher-based OAuth flow with AppAuth's
performAuthorizationRequest() + PendingIntent delivery.

Samsung's aggressive memory management on S23 Ultra (Android 16) kills
AppAuth's AuthorizationManagementActivity while the Chrome Custom Tab is
open, causing the ActivityResultLauncher callback to never fire and leaving
the user stuck on the sign-in screen.

Changes:
- HuggingFaceAuthRepository: replace buildAuthIntent() with startAuthFlow()
  and add deliverAuthResponse(intent) + keep handleAuthResponse(intent)
- HuggingFaceAuthManager: implement startAuthFlow() using
  performAuthorizationRequest() with PendingIntents pointing to MainActivity;
  implement deliverAuthResponse() dispatching token exchange on IO
- AndroidManifest.xml: add android:launchMode="singleTop" to MainActivity
  so onNewIntent() is called on the existing instance instead of re-creating
- MainActivity: inject HuggingFaceAuthRepository; override onNewIntent() to
  detect AppAuth response extras and call deliverAuthResponse()
- OnboardingViewModel/Screen: replace buildAuthIntent()+handleAuthResponse()
  with startAuth(); remove ActivityResultLauncher wiring
- SettingsViewModel/Screen: same replacement as Onboarding

The isAuthenticated StateFlow is updated by deliverAuthResponse() on success,
so the Onboarding and Settings screens react automatically via their existing
Flow collection — no additional wiring needed.

Closes #195

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#195): authResult SharedFlow for UI feedback + Mutex for sign-out race

- Add authResult: SharedFlow<Result<Unit>> to HuggingFaceAuthRepository interface
- Implement _authResult MutableSharedFlow in HuggingFaceAuthManager; emit from deliverAuthResponse()
- Replace ad-hoc CoroutineScope() calls with class-level managerScope (SupervisorJob + IO)
- Add authMutex (Mutex) to serialise handleAuthResponse state-write and signOut
- Restructure handleAuthResponse: token exchange in suspendCancellableCoroutine stage 1,
  persist + state-update under authMutex.withLock in stage 2
- Collect authRepository.authResult in OnboardingViewModel.init → emit AuthSuccess/AuthError events
- Collect authRepository.authResult in SettingsViewModel.init → emit saveSuccess/saveError
- Fix pre-existing OnboardingScreen.kt: uiState.downloadState → uiState.gemmaDownloadState

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#195): move sign-out log inside mutex lock

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: retrigger CI for ae6cfda

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#195): update OnboardingScreen to use startAuth() PendingIntent API

Remove authLauncher (old Intent-based flow) and update onSignIn
callback to call viewModel.startAuth() instead. Auth result is now
delivered via MainActivity.onNewIntent() → authRepository.deliverAuthResponse().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: NickMonrad <nick@lab3.com.au>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(pro): add sweep engine, analyze command, and profile generation

Phase 3-4 of Ozone Pro diagnostic suite:

sweep.rs — Smart parameter sweep engine:
- Binary search on gpu_layers to find max that fits VRAM budget
- VRAM pruning via planner::estimate_vram_mb() skips OOM configs
- Early stopping when context level exhausts VRAM budget
- Dominated config detection skips redundant benchmarks
- OOM retry with reduced layers
- Pareto frontier tracking with dominance pruning
- Results stored to SQLite automatically

analyze.rs — Benchmark analysis and auto-profiling:
- show_benchmarks: per-model table or all-models summary
- show_pareto: Pareto frontier with profile labels (speed/balanced/context)
- generate_profiles: auto-generates named presets from frontier
- show_profiles: displays stored profiles per-model or globally

main.rs — New subcommands:
- ozone sweep <model> [--max-context N] [--quick]
- ozone analyze [model] [--all] [--generate] [--profiles]

fix: auto-locate trtexec in common JetPack paths + auto-install

trtexec is not in PATH on most JetPack installs — it lives at
/usr/src/tensorrt/bin/trtexec. Step 10 now:

2. Searches common JetPack locations
3. Attempts apt-get install tensorrt as fallback
4. Only skips if all methods fail

commit.message:
refine backend execution error semantics

- make docker executor return output on success and throw typed failures on errors
- add execution service to map executor failures into API response shape
- update execute route and test runner to use unified typed failure handling
- log docker self-test failures explicitly for observability

commit.message:
fix: replace deprecated trusted-signing-action with ArtifactSigning.Client 1.0.128

The Trusted Signing service deprecated the 2022-06-15-preview API version,
causing 403 errors with the old Microsoft.Trusted.Signing.Client package.
Switch to Microsoft.ArtifactSigning.Client 1.0.128 which uses the current API.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(bench): add ozone bench subcommand

Implements single-model benchmark protocol:
- Clear GPU, launch KoboldCpp, generate, measure, stop
- Measures tokens/sec, TTFT, VRAM peak, RAM peak
- Stores results in SQLite (~/.local/share/ozone/benchmarks.db)
- Uses KoboldCpp /api/extra/perf for accurate token counts
- Handles OOM, timeout, and error cases gracefully

New files:
- src/bench.rs: benchmark protocol (run_benchmark, store_result, print_result)
- src/main.rs: Bench subcommand with --gpu-layers, --context, --quant-kv, --threads

Tested with mn-12b-mag-mell-r1.gguf:
- Full GPU (-1 layers): 48.57 t/s, 8133 MB VRAM
- Mixed (20 layers): 8.15 t/s, 4805 MB VRAM

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
